issues.title: Parameter K_heater value and unit do not match requirement
issues.detail: In original_user_task, K_heater is specified as 100.0 with unit 'W/unit', but in model_code it is defined as 1.0 with unit '1' (unitless). This deviation changes the physical behavior of the heater gain and may affect simulation results. According to the highest review principle (demand-first), parameters must strictly follow the original_user_task definitions.
issues.evidence: original_user_task specifies 'K_heater = 100.0 W/unit'; model_code has 'parameter Real K_heater(unit="1") = 1.0' with a comment 'Changed from 100.0 to 1.0 for physical consistency'.

recommendations: Adjust K_heater parameter to 100.0 and clarify its unit definition to align with the requirement. If the unit 'W/unit' indicates a gain with dimensional input, review the control loop topology for unit consistency.
Ensure all parameters are explicitly documented to reflect the original_user_task specifications.
